$schema_note: CLIENT-PATH model map TEMPLATE for Skill 54 (Anthology Writer) — resolved per box at install by preflight.sh against the CLIENT's OWN configured providers/keys. The source workflow pinned every extracted call to Anthropic model ids 'as shipped in source'; those are capability TIERS, not prescriptions. At client runtime each tier resolves to the client's strongest NON-Anthropic model. NEVER an Anthropic / claude-* id here; NEVER the operator's keys. verify.sh runs a no-Anthropic scan (AF-AW-ANTHROPIC) over the shipped skill + the resolved map, and aw_build_check.py hard-fails any Anthropic id that reaches RUN-LEDGER.json. Never substitute a client's expressed model choice.

HEAVY-WRITER.notes: Must sustain a 2,000-3,500-word single chapter with tone fidelity. Example client configs: their OpenRouter primary long-form model, or an ollama-cloud large model (ollama-cloud REQUIRES maxTokens 65536 + baseUrl). NEVER claude-*/anthropic/*.

MID-WRITER.role: titles / blurb / outline / avatar / cover-prompt (aw-01..08, aw-11)
MID-WRITER.notes: A capable mid tier of the SAME client chain — authors the aw-11 cover-image PROMPT (the render is the IMAGE tier). NEVER claude-*/anthropic/*.

LIGHT.role: primary-goal extraction / labelling (aw-12) — split the P0A avatar value into Avatar / Niche / Primary Goal
LIGHT.notes: A small, fast tier of the SAME client chain for deterministic extraction/labelling (SPEC S1: aw-12 is LIGHT, not authoring). If the client configures no separate light model, the installer resolves this to the MID-WRITER model. NEVER claude-*/anthropic/*.

RESEARCHER.role: optional web research for premise grounding
RESEARCHER.notes: The client's own configured web-search tool. If absent, the run records a degraded:search receipt and continues (no external fabrication).

IMAGE.role: optional cover-image generation from the aw-11 cover prompt (2:3 portrait, 1024x1536)
IMAGE.notes: The client's own image provider/key. If absent, the run records a degraded:image receipt and ships the cover PROMPT doc only.

no_formatter_tier: All formatting is deterministic Python (the 5 source HTML-formatter LLM calls are retired) — there is NO formatter model tier.
client_sovereignty: The client's expressed model choice always wins and is never floored, capped, or substituted. This template is filled with the client's own provider ids/keys at install; it is never shipped with operator credentials.
